title: A Look At Southwest Gas Holdings (SWX) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

Southwest Gas Holdings stock: key context for recent performance

Southwest Gas Holdings (SWX) has been on the radar for investors after a period of steady share price moves, with returns of 3.7% over the past month and 12.5% over the past 3 months.

See our latest analysis for Southwest Gas Holdings.

Looking beyond the recent move, Southwest Gas Holdings’ 1-day share price return of 2.18% sits within a broader pattern, with a 90-day share price return of 12.46% and a 1-year total shareholder return of 33.73%. This indicates that momentum has been building rather than fading.

If you are comparing Southwest Gas with other utilities exposed to energy infrastructure themes, it can help to widen the lens and look at 29 power grid technology and infrastructure stocks.

With Southwest Gas shares already up strongly over 1 and 3 years and trading close to the average analyst price target, the key question is whether the current valuation leaves meaningful upside or whether the market is already pricing in future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 1.6% Undervalued

With Southwest Gas Holdings closing at $90.92 versus a narrative fair value of $92.43, the widely followed view is that the shares sit slightly below assessed value, anchored on steady growth and regulated earnings assumptions.

Robust customer growth facilitated by ongoing population and economic expansion in the Southwest, with 40,000 new meter connections in the last 12 months, suggests extended demand for natural gas in core service territories, directly underpinning long-term revenue and earnings growth.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what justifies paying close to fair value for a regulated utility? The narrative leans heavily on projected revenue expansion, rising margins, and a richer earnings profile several years out. Want to see which specific growth and profitability assumptions sit behind that conclusion?

Result: Fair Value of $92.43 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this hinges on natural gas remaining attractive as decarbonization and electrification gather pace, and on large projects like Great Basin avoiding delays or cost overruns.

Find out about the key risks to this Southwest Gas Holdings narrative.

Another View: Multiples Paint A Richer Picture

While the narrative fair value suggests Southwest Gas Holdings is only 1.6% undervalued, its current P/E of 27.5x compares with a fair ratio of 22.2x, the peer average of 18.2x and the global gas utilities average of 14.9x. That gap points to valuation risk rather than a clear bargain. How comfortable are you paying this premium?

Story Continues

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.NYSE:SWX P/E Ratio as at Apr 2026

title: OTTR, HTO draw highest short interest in utilities sector; BIP and SRE see lowest short interest
content:
Within the utilities sector (XLU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLU]), short sellers remain heavily concentrated in electric utilities, with several electricity producers and multi-utility companies ranking among the most heavily shorted stocks in the market as of end-March.

Otter Tail (OTTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OTTR]) and H2O America (HTO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HTO]) recorded the highest short interest among utilities stocks with a market capitalization of over $2B.

The utilities sector (XLU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLU]) comprises companies involved in services like electricity, natural gas, and water, among other services.

Otter Tail (OTTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OTTR]) was the most shorted stock in the utilities sector, with short interest at 12.92% of its shares outstanding, followed by H2O America (HTO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HTO]) at 12.30%, reflecting elevated bearish positioning in both stocks.

Otter Tail (OTTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OTTR]) was rated a Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OTTR/ratings/quant-ratings] by Seeking Alpha Quant ratings. However, Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts are bullish and rated OTTR a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OTTR/ratings/author-ratings].

Seeking Alpha Quant ratings rated [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HTO/ratings/quant-ratings] HTO as a Sell. However, Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish and have rated it a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HTO/ratings/sell-side-ratings] and Buy, respectively.

At the other end of the spectrum, Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIP]) and Sempra (SRE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SRE]) were among the least shorted utilities stocks, with short interest of 0.20% and 1.36%, respectively.

THE TOP FIVE MOST SHORTED UTILITIES STOCKS WITH A MARKET CAPITALIZATION ABOVE $2B (AS A PERCENTAGE OF SHARES OUTSTANDING) ARE

 	* Otter Tail (OTTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OTTR]) 12.92% short interest
 	* H2O America (HTO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HTO]) 12.30%
 	* Oklo (OKLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKLO]) 12.18%
 	* Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HE]) 11.89%
 	* Black Hills (BKH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BKH]) 9.26%

THE FIVE LEAST SHORTED UTILITIES STOCKS, WITH A MARKET CAPITALIZATION ABOVE $2B, ARE:

 	* PG&E Corporation (PCG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PCG]) 1.89% short interest
 	* Southwest Gas (SWX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SWX]) 1.77%
 	* MGE Energy (MGEE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MGEE]) 1.68%
 	* Sempra (SRE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SRE]) 1.36%
 	* Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIP]) 0.20%
